title: Authors and Citation
text: Stefanos Balaskas. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Balaskas S (2026). gp3ml: Leakage-Safe Predictive Validation Gazepoint Research. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3ml/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gp3ml: Leakage-Safe Predictive Validation for Gazepoint Research},   author = {Stefanos Balaskas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3ml/}, }

title: gp3ml
text: gp3ml R package cautious development leakage-resistant group-aware predictive validation using Gazepoint-derived research data.

title: gp3ml
text: first development stages focus : participant-, trial-, stimulus-leakage auditing; explicit generalization-target declarations; outcome, predictor, identifier-role validation; feature-provenance manifests; grouped train/test splitting; grouped nested resampling plans; fold-overlap balance diagnostics; calibration uncertainty assessment; model-card reproducibility reporting. package intended generic wrapper around existing machine-learning frameworks.

title: gp3ml
text: Participant overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization new participants. Stimulus overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization unseen stimuli. Preprocessing feature selection must estimated inside relevant resampling folds. package examples tests use deterministic synthetic data.

title: gp3ml
text: audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage() audits already-defined analysis assessment partitions predictive evaluation. checks: compatibility declared generalization target; participant, participant-trial, stimulus overlap; outcome declared identifiers included predictors; declared target-derived post-outcome predictors; exact row overlap repeated predictor profiles; duplicated rows missing grouping identifiers; identifier-like predictor names requiring manual review. returned audit overall pass, review, fail status, complete check table, non-passing issue table, partition summaries. Audit tables can exported using write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv(). audit identifies structural risks visible supplied partitions declared variable roles. establish preprocessing feature selection estimated within resampling folds; safeguards require separate provenance resampling infrastructure.
code: analysis <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P01", "P02"),   trial_id = c("T01", "T02"),   stimulus_id = c("S01", "S02"),   outcome = c(0, 1),   fixation_duration = c(210, 245) )  assessment <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P03", "P04"),   trial_id = c("T03", "T04"),   stimulus_id = c("S03", "S04"),   outcome = c(1, 0),   fixation_duration = c(275, 230) )  audit <- audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis = analysis,   assessment = assessment,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = "fixation_duration",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants" )  audit

title: gp3ml
text: create_gazepoint_feature_manifest() records declared origin, transformation, availability, role, preprocessing scope intended predictor. validate_gazepoint_feature_manifest() checks whether manifest contains sufficient provenance metadata identifies predictors declared outcome-derived, post-outcome, unavailable prediction time, identifiers, incompatible required fold-local preprocessing. Validation returns overall pass, review, fail status, together complete check issue tables. manifest validation tables can exported using write_gazepoint_feature_manifest_csv(). manifest records declared provenance. independently verify preprocessing executed within stated partition resampling scope.
code: manifest <- create_gazepoint_feature_manifest(   features = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   scientific_source = c(     "Gazepoint fixation export",     "Gazepoint all-gaze export"   ),   source_table = c("fixations", "all_gaze"),   transformation = c(     "Trial-level mean",     "Baseline-adjusted change"   ),   availability_stage = "during_exposure",   prediction_time_available = TRUE,   preprocessing_scope = c("none", "resampling_fold"),   fold_local_required = c(FALSE, TRUE) )  validation <- validate_gazepoint_feature_manifest(manifest) validation

title: gp3ml
text: split_gazepoint_ml_data() creates deterministic analysis assessment partitions preserve grouping unit implied explicit predictive-generalization target. passing feature-provenance manifest required splitting, resulting partitions checked using leakage audit. Supported generalization targets : new_trials_known_participants; new_participants; new_stimuli; new_participants_and_new_stimuli. simultaneous participant stimulus generalization, rows belonging one held-dimension placed excluded partition. preserves strict separation analysis assessment participant–stimulus blocks retaining complete source-row accounting. validate_gazepoint_ml_split() checks partition structure, source-row accounting, provenance status, embedded leakage audit. Split assignments, summaries, validation checks, materialized partitions can exported using write_gazepoint_ml_split_csv(). splitter creates one deterministic holdout split. perform preprocessing, automated feature selection, cross-validation, nested resampling, model fitting.
code: split_data <- expand.grid(   participant_id = sprintf("P%02d", 1:8),   trial_id = sprintf("T%02d", 1:4),   KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  split_data$outcome <- as.integer(   seq_len(nrow(split_data)) %% 2L )  split_data$fixation_duration <-   200 + seq_len(nrow(split_data))  split_data$pupil_change <-   seq_len(nrow(split_data)) / 100  split <- split_gazepoint_ml_data(   data = split_data,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c(     "fixation_duration",     "pupil_change"   ),   feature_manifest = manifest,   generalization_target = "new_participants",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   assessment_prop = 0.25,   seed = 17 )  split split$validation

title: gp3ml
text: create_gazepoint_group_folds() creates deterministic repeated grouped V-fold plans explicit generalization targets supported holdout splitter. passing feature-provenance manifest required, every analysis-assessment pair checked using leakage audit. Supported targets new trials among known participants, new participants, new stimuli, simultaneous new-participant new-stimulus generalization. simultaneous participant stimulus generalization, v may contain separate participant stimulus fold counts. Crossed assessment blocks preserve strict separation dimensions, cross-block rows explicitly assigned excluded partition. validate_gazepoint_group_folds() checks fold counts, source-row accounting, assessment coverage, materialized partitions, feature provenance, embedded leakage audits. audit_gazepoint_group_folds() aggregates fold-level audits. Assignments, summaries, validation tables, audit tables, optionally materialized partitions can exported using write_gazepoint_group_folds_csv(). fold planner perform preprocessing, automated feature selection, tuning, nested resampling, model fitting.
code: folds <- create_gazepoint_group_folds(   data = split_data,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c(     "fixation_duration",     "pupil_change"   ),   feature_manifest = manifest,   generalization_target = "new_participants",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   v = 4,   repeats = 2,   seed = 17 )  folds folds$validation folds$audit

title: gp3ml
text: diagnose_gazepoint_group_folds() evaluates balance, coverage, outcome representation, exclusions existing grouped resampling plan. Diagnostics remain downstream fold construction modify assignments fit models. validate_gazepoint_fold_diagnostics() reports pass, review, fail findings fold counts, partition accounting, non-empty partitions, assessment coverage, fold-size imbalance, group presence, outcome-level presence, embedded leakage audits, source-plan status. crossed new-participant new-stimulus designs, exclusion_summary reports rows intentionally excluded belong one held dimension rather strict crossed assessment block. diagnostic tables can exported review reporting: Diagnostics perform preprocessing, automated feature selection, hyperparameter tuning, nested resampling, model fitting.
code: fold_diagnostics <- diagnose_gazepoint_group_folds(folds)  fold_diagnostics fold_diagnostics$repeat_metrics head(fold_diagnostics$outcome_balance) head(fold_diagnostics$assessment_coverage) write_gazepoint_fold_diagnostics_csv(   fold_diagnostics,   directory = "resampling-diagnostics",   prefix = "gazepoint_folds" )

title: gp3ml
text: package support person identification, health inference, protected-attribute prediction, direct inference emotion, stress, mental state, cognition, comprehension, personality, deception, intent. See: GOVERNANCE.md PROHIBITED-USE.md

title: gp3ml
text: Version 0.0.0.9000 includes structured leakage auditing, feature-provenance manifests, deterministic group-aware holdout splitting, repeated grouped resampling plans, fold validation, aggregated leakage auditing, machine-readable CSV export. model-training interface, automated feature selection, tuning, nested resampling, preprocessing engine implemented.

One "new_trials_known_participants", "new_participants", "new_stimuli", "new_participants_and_new_stimuli". target_derived Character vector columns known derived directly outcome. post_outcome Character vector columns measured constructed outcome became available.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: object class gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit. object contains overall status, partition summary, complete check table, machine-readable table non-passing issues.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
text: overall status "fail" least one failing check present, "review" failing checks present least one review item present, "pass" otherwise. participant_id supplied, trial overlap evaluated using composite participant-trial units. permits trial labels "T01" reused different participants without treated leakage. Without participant_id, trial_id assumed globally unique. audit can identify structural leakage visible supplied partitions declared variable roles. prove preprocessing feature selection estimated inside resampling folds. operations require separate provenance resampling safeguards. function determine whether outcome scientifically ethically appropriate. uses remain subject package governance prohibited-use statements.

title: Audit leakage between predictive-analysis partitions — audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage
code: analysis <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P01", "P01", "P02", "P02"),   trial_id = c("T01", "T02", "T03", "T04"),   stimulus_id = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04"),   outcome = c(0, 1, 0, 1),   fixation_duration = c(210, 240, 225, 260),   pupil_change = c(0.10, 0.16, 0.12, 0.18) )  assessment <- data.frame(   participant_id = c("P03", "P03", "P04", "P04"),   trial_id = c("T05", "T06", "T07", "T08"),   stimulus_id = c("S05", "S06", "S07", "S08"),   outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0),   fixation_duration = c(275, 230, 290, 245),   pupil_change = c(0.21, 0.11, 0.24, 0.14) )  audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage(   analysis = analysis,   assessment = assessment,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants" ) #> <gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit> #> Overall status: PASS #> Generalization target: new_participants #> Rows: 4 analysis; 4 assessment #> Non-passing checks: 0 #> No leakage issues were detected by the implemented checks.

code: create_gazepoint_feature_manifest(   features,   scientific_source = NA_character_,   source_table = NA_character_,   transformation = "none",   availability_stage = "unknown",   prediction_time_available = NA,   outcome_derived = FALSE,   post_outcome = FALSE,   identifier = FALSE,   preprocessing_scope = "unknown",   fold_local_required = NA,   reviewer_notes = "" )

title: Create a Gazepoint feature-provenance manifest — create_gazepoint_feature_manifest
text: features Character vector unique feature names. scientific_source Scientific measurement source feature. source_table Source export, table, object feature. transformation Description transformation used construct feature. availability_stage Availability stage feature. One "pre_exposure", "during_exposure", "post_exposure_pre_outcome", "at_prediction", "post_outcome", "unknown". prediction_time_available Logical vector indicating whether feature available intended prediction time. outcome_derived Logical vector indicating whether feature derived directly indirectly outcome. post_outcome Logical vector indicating whether feature measured constructed outcome became available. identifier Logical vector indicating whether feature identifier row-location variable. preprocessing_scope Scope data-dependent preprocessing estimated. One "none", "global", "analysis_partition", "resampling_fold", "unknown". fold_local_required Logical vector indicating whether preprocessing feature must estimated separately inside resampling fold. reviewer_notes Optional reviewer-facing notes.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added diagnose_gazepoint_group_folds() structured diagnostics grouped resampling plans. Added fold-level repeat-level summaries analysis, assessment, excluded row counts. Added assessment fold-size imbalance ratios configurable review failure thresholds. Added group-balance summaries participant, participant-trial, stimulus units. Added assessment-coverage diagnostics verifying source row assessed exactly per repeat. Added categorical outcome-level representation continuous numeric outcome summaries fold partition. Added explicit exclusion summaries crossed participant--stimulus resampling designs. Added validate_gazepoint_fold_diagnostics() structured pass, review, fail findings. Added print methods write_gazepoint_fold_diagnostics_csv() eight machine-readable diagnostic tables. Added deterministic regression coverage across four supported generalization targets. milestone introduce preprocessing, feature selection, tuning, nested resampling, model fitting.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added create_gazepoint_group_folds() deterministic repeated group-aware V-fold plans. Required explicit predictive-generalization target passing feature-provenance manifest fold construction. Added support new trials among known participants, new participants, new stimuli, simultaneous new-participant new-stimulus generalization. Added crossed participant-stimulus assessment blocks explicit accounting excluded cross-block rows. Added validate_gazepoint_group_folds() fold counts, source-row accounting, assessment coverage, partition consistency, provenance, leakage-audit validation. Added audit_gazepoint_group_folds() aggregating embedded fold-level leakage audits. Added print methods write_gazepoint_group_folds_csv() machine-readable summaries optional fold export. Added deterministic synthetic tests supported targets, repeated folds, invalid requests, auditing, CSV output. preprocessing, automated feature selection, tuning, nested resampling, model fitting introduced.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added split_gazepoint_ml_data() deterministic group-aware analysis assessment partitions. Required explicit predictive-generalization target passing feature-provenance manifest splitting. Added support new trials among known participants, new participants, new stimuli, simultaneous new-participant new-stimulus generalization. Preserved participant-trial, participant, stimulus groups according declared target. Added strict participant–stimulus block separation explicit accounting excluded cross-block rows. Added validate_gazepoint_ml_split() source-row, partition-structure, provenance, leakage-audit checks. Added print methods write_gazepoint_ml_split_csv() machine-readable export. Added deterministic synthetic tests covering supported generalization targets intentionally invalid requests. preprocessing, automated feature selection, resampling, model fitting introduced.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added create_gazepoint_feature_manifest() recording predictor origins, transformations, availability stages, roles, preprocessing scopes. Added validate_gazepoint_feature_manifest() structured pass, review, fail results. Added checks outcome-derived, post-outcome, identifier, prediction-time-unavailable features. Added checks incomplete provenance preprocessing incompatible declared fold-local requirements. Added write_gazepoint_feature_manifest_csv() exporting manifests validation tables. Added deterministic synthetic tests safe, incomplete, intentionally unsafe manifests.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Added audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage() structured auditing already-defined analysis assessment partitions. Added explicit checks participant, participant-trial, stimulus compatibility declared generalization targets. Added checks exact-row overlap, duplicated rows, repeated predictor profiles, identifier predictors, target-derived predictors, post-outcome predictors. Added pass, review, fail audit statuses complete check, issue, partition-summary tables. Added write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv() machine-readable audit export. Added deterministic synthetic tests covering clean intentionally contaminated partitions.

title: gp3ml 0.0.0.9000
text: Created minimal R package structure. Added explicit model-governance prohibited-use statements. Restricted initial scope validation governance. Confirmed examples tests use deterministic synthetic data . model-training functions implemented.
